that score-based merge sorts globally by score.\n        // With global IDF (simulated here by consistent scores across shards),\n        // doc with highest score should rank first regardless of shard.\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                // Shard 0: low scores\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"doc-low-1\", 0.3, 0),\n                        make_hit(\"doc-low-2\", 0.2, 0),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n                // Shard 1: high scores (these should rank higher)\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"doc-high-1\", 0.9, 1),\n                        make_hit(\"doc-high-2\", 0.8, 1),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 4);\n\n        // Should be sorted by score descending globally\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-high-1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-high-2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-low-1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[3].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-low-2\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_tie_breaking() {\n        // Test deterministic tie-breaking on primary key when scores are equal.\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"zebra\", 0.5, 0),\n                        make_hit(\"apple\", 0.5, 0),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        // Both docs have same score, should tie-break alphabetically\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"apple\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"zebra\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_offset_limit() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![\n                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.8, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc3\", 0.7, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc4\", 0.6, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc5\", 0.5, 0),\n                ],\n                100,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 1,\n            limit: 2,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_preserves_score_when_requested() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.9)\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_strips_score_when_not_requested() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test: skewed corpus scenario with global-IDF preflight.\n    ///\n    /// This simulates the scenario described in P12.OP4:\n    /// - Shard 0 has 1000 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 100 (df=100)\n    /// - Shard 1 has 100 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 50 (df=50)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF:\n    /// - Local IDF(shard 0) = log((1000-100+0.5)/(100+0.5)+1) ≈ 2.2\n    /// - Local IDF(shard 1) = log((100-50+0.5)/(50+0.5)+1) ≈ 0.7\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF:\n    /// - Global N = 1100, global df = 150\n    /// - Global IDF = log((1100-150+0.5)/(150+0.5)+1) ≈ 1.8\n    ///\n    /// A document with tf=3 for \"rust\" in each shard:\n    /// - Shard 0 score (local IDF): ~3 * 2.2 = 6.6\n    /// - Shard 1 score (local IDF): ~3 * 0.7 = 2.1\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF, shard 0 doc ranks higher despite shard 1 having\n    /// much higher term density (50/100 vs 100/1000).\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF, both shards use IDF=1.8, and the doc with higher\n    /// term density (normalized by document length) ranks correctly.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_skewed_corpus_integration() {\n        // Simulate global IDF applied (scores are now comparable)\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n        // Doc in large shard with high term frequency but low density\n        let doc_large_shard = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-large\",\n            \"title\": \"Rust in Large Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.75, // After global-IDF normalization\n        });\n\n        // Doc in small shard with lower term frequency but high density\n        let doc_small_shard = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-small\",\n            \"title\": \"Rust in Small Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.85, // After global-IDF normalization\n        });\n\n        // With global IDF, the small shard doc should rank higher\n        // because its term density is higher (50/100 vs 100/1000)\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_large_shard],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1000,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                    }),\n                },\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_small_shard],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n                    }),\n                },\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n\n        // Small shard doc should rank first (higher score after global IDF)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-small\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-large\");\n\n        // Scores should be preserved\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.85)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.75)\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test that demonstrates the failure mode without global IDF.\n    ///\n    /// This shows what happens when scores are NOT comparable across shards:\n    /// score-based merge produces incorrect rankings, while RRF at least\n    /// produces consistent (though not optimal) results.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_without_global_idf_fails() {\n        // Simulate the bug: shard-local IDF produces incomparable scores\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n        // Shard 0: large shard, inflated local IDF (shard has term rarity)\n        let doc_shard0 = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-inflated\",\n            \"title\": \"Document in Large Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.95, // Inflated due to high local IDF\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: small shard, deflated local IDF (term is common here)\n        let doc_shard1 = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-deflated\",\n            \"title\": \"Document in Small Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.60, // Deflated due to low local IDF\n        });\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_shard0],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 10000,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n                    }),\n                },\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_shard1],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n                    }),\n                },\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        // Without global IDF, score-based merge trusts the inflated scores\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-inflated\");\n\n        // This is WRONG: doc-deflated has much higher term density but\n        // ranks lower due to shard-local IDF skew.\n        //\n        // The solution is the preflight phase (dfs_query_then_fetch_search)\n        // which computes global IDF so scores are comparable.\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_default_impl() {\n        let via_default: ScoreMergeStrategy = Default::default();\n        let via_constructor = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        assert_eq!(via_default.name(), via_constructor.name());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_empty_input() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 0);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // P12.OP4 RRF skew validation\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// Validates the P12.OP4 finding: RRF merge with extreme shard skew\n    /// produces incorrect global rankings because it gives equal weight\n    /// to all shards regardless of their size.\n    ///\n    /// Scenario: 10 shards where shard 0 has 93K docs (93%) and shard 9\n    /// has 10 docs (0.01%). RRF assigns identical scores to rank-0 hits\n    /// from all shards, so a mediocre hit from the tiny shard ranks\n    /// equally with the best hit from the dominant shard.\n    ///\n    /// Benchmark result (10K queries, skewed corpus):\n    ///   Score merge:   τ = 0.79  (95% CI [0.787, 0.801]) — FAIL\n    ///   RRF merge:     τ = 0.14  (95% CI [0.134, 0.140]) — FAIL\n    ///   DFS preflight: τ = 0.98  (95% CI [0.982, 0.982]) — PASS\n    ///\n    /// Conclusion: RRF alone does NOT solve cross-shard comparability.\n    /// Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch) is required.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_equal_weight_problem() {\n        // Shard 0 (dominant): doc-best should be the global #1 result.\n        // It has the highest score and appears in the shard with 93% of docs.\n        let shard_dominant = make_shard_response(\n            vec![\n                make_hit(\"doc-best\", 0.95, 0),      // True global #1\n                make_hit(\"doc-good\", 0.90, 0),       // True global #2\n                make_hit(\"doc-ok\", 0.85, 0),         // True global #3\n                make_hit(\"doc-mediocre\", 0.70, 0),   // True global #4\n                make_hit(\"doc-weak\", 0.60, 0),       // True global #5\n            ],\n            93_000,\n            10,\n        );\n\n        // Shard 9 (tiny, 10 docs): due to local IDF skew, irrelevant docs\n        // can appear at rank 0 with inflated local scores.\n        let shard_tiny = make_shard_response(\n            vec![\n                make_hit(\"doc-irrelevant\", 0.98, 9), // Inflated local IDF → high score\n                make_hit(\"doc-noise\", 0.92, 9),\n            ],\n            10,\n            2,\n        );\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: vec![shard_dominant, shard_tiny],\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 10,\n                client_requested_score: true,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let ids: Vec<_> = result\n            .hits\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()))\n            .collect();\n\n        // RRF gives equal rank weight to both shards.\n        // Rank 0 from dominant shard: 1/61 ≈ 0.0164\n        // Rank 0 from tiny shard:     1/61 ≈ 0.0164 (identical!)\n        //\n        // Tie-breaking falls to primary key (alphabetical), NOT relevance.\n        // doc-best and doc-irrelevant both get RRF score 1/61.\n        // Alphabetically: doc-best < doc-irrelevant → doc-best wins the tie.\n        //\n        // But doc-irrelevant still ranks above doc-good, doc-ok, doc-mediocre,\n        // and doc-weak — all of which are more relevant globally.\n        assert_eq!(ids[0], \"doc-best\");      // Tie-break win (alphabetical)\n        assert_eq!(ids[1], \"doc-irrelevant\"); // Tie-break loss, but still rank 2!\n\n        // doc-irrelevant (globally irrelevant) ranks ABOVE doc-good (global #2)\n        let irrelevant_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-irrelevant\").unwrap();\n        let good_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-good\").unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            irrelevant_pos < good_pos,\n            \"RRF skew bug: irrelevant doc (pos {}) ranks above doc-good (pos {})\",\n            irrelevant_pos,\n            good_pos,\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Computes Kendall tau between two rankings (document ID lists).\n    /// Used to validate merge quality against ground truth.\n    fn kendall_tau(ranking1: &[String], ranking2: &[String]) -> f64 {\n        let pos1: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking1\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n            .collect();\n        let pos2: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking2\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n            .collect();\n\n        let common: Vec<&str> = pos1\n            .keys()\n            .filter(|k| pos2.contains_key(*k))\n            .map(|k| *k)\n            .collect();\n\n        if common.len() < 2 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n\n        let r2_positions: Vec<usize> = common.iter().map(|id| pos2[id]).collect();\n        let (_, discordant) = count_inversions(&r2_positions);\n        let n = common.len();\n        let total = n * (n - 1) / 2;\n        let concordant = total - discordant;\n        (concordant as f64 - discordant as f64) / total as f64\n    }\n\n    fn count_inversions(arr: &[usize]) -> (Vec<usize>, usize) {\n        if arr.len() <= 1 {\n            return (arr.to_vec(), 0);\n        }\n        let mid = arr.len() / 2;\n        let (left, inv_l) = count_inversions(&arr[..mid]);\n        let (right, inv_r) = count_inversions(&arr[mid..]);\n\n        let mut merged = Vec::with_capacity(arr.len());\n        let mut inv = inv_l + inv_r;\n        let (mut i, mut j) = (0, 0);\n\n        while i < left.len() && j < right.len() {\n            if left[i] <= right[j] {\n                merged.push(left[i]);\n                i += 1;\n            } else {\n                merged.push(right[j]);\n                inv += left.len() - i;\n                j += 1;\n            }\n        }\n        merged.extend_from_slice(&left[i..]);\n        merged.extend_from_slice(&right[j..]);\n        (merged, inv)\n    }\n\n    /// End-to-end validation: RRF merge on skewed shards produces τ < 0.95\n    /// against ground truth (single-index ranking).\n    ///\n    /// This is a scaled-down version of the 10K-query Python benchmark.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_tau_below_threshold() {\n        let k = DEFAULT_RRF_K;\n\n        // Build 5 shards with skewed sizes: [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000]\n        // Ground truth: all 17600 docs in one index, sorted by score.\n        let mut all_docs: Vec<(String, f64)> = Vec::new();\n        let mut shard_docs: Vec<Vec<(String, f64)>> = vec![vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![]];\n        let shard_sizes = [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000];\n\n        let mut rng = simple_rng(42);\n        for (shard_id, &size) in shard_sizes.iter().enumerate() {\n            for i in 0..size {\n                // Deterministic pseudo-random scores\n                let score = fake_bm25_score(shard_id, i, &mut rng);\n                let doc_id = format!(\"s{}-d{:06}\", shard_id, i);\n                all_docs.push((doc_id.clone(), score));\n                shard_docs[shard_id].push((doc_id, score));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Ground truth: global sort by score descending\n        all_docs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n        let ground_truth: Vec<String> = all_docs.iter().take(100).map(|(id, _)| id.clone()).collect();\n\n        // Per-shard: sort locally (simulates local BM25 with local IDF)\n        for docs in &mut shard_docs {\n            docs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n        }\n\n        // RRF merge using the actual Rust merger\n        let shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_docs\n            .iter()\n            .map(|docs| {\n                let hits: Vec<Value> = docs\n                    .iter()\n                    .take(200)\n                    .map(|(id, score)| {\n                        json!({\n                            \"id\": id,\n                            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n                        })\n                    })\n                    .collect();\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": hits,\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": docs.len(),\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                    }),\n                }\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::new(k);\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: shard_pages,\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 100,\n                client_requested_score: true,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let rrf_ranking: Vec<String> = result\n            .hits\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(String::from))\n            .collect();\n\n        let tau = kendall_tau(&ground_truth, &rrf_ranking);\n\n        // RRF with skewed shards should produce τ well below 0.95.\n        assert!(\n            tau < 0.95,\n            \"RRF tau = {:.4}, expected < 0.95 with skewed shards\",\n            tau,\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Simple deterministic PRNG for reproducible test scores.\n    fn simple_rng(seed: u64) -> impl FnMut() -> f64 {\n        let mut state = seed;\n        move || {\n            state = state.wrapping_mul(6_364_136_223_846_793_005).wrapping_add(1);\n            (state >> 33) as f64 / (1u64 << 31) as f64\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Simulates a BM25-like score with shard-dependent IDF skew.\n    fn fake_bm25_score(shard_id: usize, _doc_idx: usize, rng: &mut impl FnMut() -> f64) -> f64 {\n        let tf = 1.0 + rng() * 10.0;\n        // Larger shards have lower IDF for common terms (simulating skew)\n        let shard_weight = match shard_id {\n            0 => 0.3,\n            1 => 0.5,\n            2 => 0.7,\n            3 => 0.9,\n            4 => 1.0,\n            _ => 0.5,\n        };\n        tf * shard_weight + rng() * 0.5\n    }\n}\n","numLines":2018,"startLine":1,"totalLines":2018}}}

resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n75\t            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n76\t                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n77\t            }\n78\t        }\n79\t\n80\t        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n81\t            total_length / total_docs as f64\n82\t        } else {\n83\t            0.0\n84\t        };\n85\t\n86\t        let n = total_docs as f64;\n87\t        let terms = term_df\n88\t            .into_iter()\n89\t            .map(|(term, df)| {\n90\t                let idf = if df == 0 {\n91\t                    0.0\n92\t                } else {\n93\t                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n94\t                };\n95\t                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n96\t            })\n97\t            .collect();\n98\t\n99\t        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n100\t    }\n101\t}\n102\t\n103\t// SearchRequest {\n341\t    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n342\t    ///\n343\t    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n344\t    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n345\t    /// data to apply pagination.\n346\t    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n347\t        let mut body = query_seq,\n456\t        index,\n457\t        floor,\n458\t        version_checker,\n459\t    )?;\n460\t\n461\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n462\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count   .iter()\n467\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n468\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n469\t                version >= floor\n470\t            })\n471\t            .collect();\n472\t\n473\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n474\t            return None;\n475\t        }\n476\t\n477\t        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n478\t }\n484\t        } else {\n485\t            eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone()\n486\t        };\n487\t\n488\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected.clone());\n489\t hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n497\t    })\n498\t}\n499\t\n500\t/// Plan search scatter for a specific replica group (plan §13.6 session pinning).\n501\t///\n502\t/// Used when a session  replica_selector: &ReplicaSelector,\n553\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n554\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n555\t\n556\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n557\t        Some(g) => g,\n558\t        None => {\n559\t            return ScatterPlan {\n560\t                chosen_group,\n561\t                target_shards: Vec::new(),\n562\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(),\n563\t                deadline_ms: 0,\n564\t                hedging_eligible: false,\n565\t            };\n566\t        }\n567\t    };\n568\t\n569\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n570\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n571\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n572\t        if replicas.is_empty() {\n573\t            continue;\n574\t        }\n575\t\n576\t        // Use adaptive selection to pick the best replica\n577\t        let selected = match replica_selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n578\t            Some(node) => node,\n579\t            None => {\n580\t                // Fallback to round-robin if selector returns None\n581\t                replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n582\t            }\n583\t        };\n584\t\n585\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n586\t    }\n587\t\n588\t    ScatterPlan {\n589\t        chosen_group,\n590\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n591\t        shard_to_node,\n592\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n593\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n594\t    }\n595\t}\n596\t\n597\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\n598\tpub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n599\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n600\t    client: &C,\n601\t    req: SearchRequest,\n602\t    topology: &Topology,\n603\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n604\t) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n605\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n606\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n607\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n608\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n609\t        }\n610\t    }\n611\t    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n612\t\n613\t    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n614\t    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n615\t    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n616\t\n617\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n618\t    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n619\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n620\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n621\t            None => {\n622\t                for shard_id in shards {\n623\t                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n624\t                }\n625\t                continue;\n626\t            }\n627\t        };\n628\t        let client_ref = client;\n629\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n630\t        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n631\t        let shard_count = shards.len();\n632\t        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n633\t        let span = info_span!(\n634\t            \"scatter_node\",\n635\t            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n636\t            address = %node.address,\n637\t            shard_count = shard_count,\n638\t        );\n639\t        tasks.push(async move {\n640\t            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n641\t            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n642\t        }.instrument(span));\n643\t    }\n644\t\n645\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n646\t\n647\t    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n648\t        match result {\n649\t            Ok(body) => {\n650\t                for _shard_id in shards {\n651\t                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n652\t                }\n653\t            }\n654\t            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n655\t                deadline_exceeded = true;\n656\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n657\t            }\n658\t            Err(e) => {\n659\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n660\t            }\n661\t        }\n662\t    }\n663\t\n664\t    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n665\t\n666\t    match policy {\n667\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n668\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n669\t                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n670\t            }\n671\t        }\n672\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n673\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n674\t            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n675\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n676\t                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n677\t                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n678\t\n679\t                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n680\t                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n681\t\n682\t                    // Try each other replica group\n683\t                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n684\t                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n685\t                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n686\t                        }\n687\t\n688\t                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n689\t                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n690\t                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n691\t                                continue;\n692\t                            }\n693\t\n694\t                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n695\t                            for node_id in replicas {\n696\t                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n697\t                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n698\t                                        Ok(body) => {\n699\t                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n700\t                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n701\t                                            break;\n702\t                                        }\n703\t                                        Err(_) => {\n704\t                                            continue; // Try next replica\n705\t                                        }\n706\t                                    }\n707\t                                }\n708\t                            }\n709\t\n710\t                            if fallback_succeeded {\n711\t                                break; // Found a working replica\n712\t                            }\n713\t                        }\n714\t                    }\n715\t\n716\t                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n717\t                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n718\t                    }\n719\t                }\n720\t\n721\t                // Merge fallback results\n722\t                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n723\t\n724\t                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n725\t                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n726\t                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n727\t                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n728\t                        shard_pages,\n729\t                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n730\t                        partial: false,\n731\t                        deadline_exceeded,\n732\t                    });\n733\t                } else {\n734\t                    // Some shards still failed\n735\t                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n736\t                }\n737\t            }\n738\t        }\n739\t    }\n740\t\n741\t    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n742\t}\n743\t\n744\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n745\tpub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n746\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n747\t    client: &C,\n748\t    req: SearchRequest,\n749\t    topology: &Topology,\n750\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n751\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n752\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n753\t    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n754\t\n755\t    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n756\t    if scatter_result.partial {\n757\t        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n758\t            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n759\t                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n760\t            });\n761\t        }\n762\t    }\n763\t\n764\t    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n765\t    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n766\t\n767\t    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n768\t        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n769\t        offset: req.offset,\n770\t        limit: req.limit,\n771\t        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n772\t        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n773\t        failed_shards,\n774\t    };\n775\t\n776\t    // Span for the merge operation\n777\t    let _span = info_span!(\n778\t        \"merge\",\n779\t        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n780\t        offset = req.offset,\n781\t        limit = req.limit,\n782\t    ).entered();\n783\t\n784\t    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n785\t}\n786\t\n787\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n788\t// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n789\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n790\t\n791\t/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\n792\tpub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n793\t    match query {\n794\t        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n795\t            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n796\t            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n797\t            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n798\t                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n799\t                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n800\t            }\n801\t            terms\n802\t        }\n803\t        _ => Vec::new(),\n804\t    }\n805\t}\n806\t\n807\t/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n808\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\n809\tpub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n810\t    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n811\t    client: &C,\n812\t    req: &PreflightRequest,\n813\t    topology: &Topology,\n814\t) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n815\t    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n816\t        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n820\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n821\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n822\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n823\t        }\n824\t    }\n825\t\n826\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n827\t    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n828\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n829\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n830\t            None => continue,\n831\t        };\n832\t        let client_ref = client;\n833\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n834\t        let nid = node_id.clone();\n835\t        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n836\t    }\n837\t\n838\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n839\t    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n840\t    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n841\t}\n842\t\n843\t/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n844\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n845\tpub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n846\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n847\t    client: &C,\n848\t    req: SearchRequest,\n849\t    topology: &Topology,\n850\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n851\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n852\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n853\t    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n854\t        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n855\t        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n856\t        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n857\t    };\n858\t    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n859\t    let mut search_req = req;\n860\t    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n861\t    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n862\t}\n863\t\n864\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n865\t// Mock client\n866\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n867\t\n868\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n869\tpub struct MockNodeClient {\n870\t    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n871\t    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n872\t    pub write_responses: HashMap<NodeId, WriteResponse>,\n873\t    pub fetch_responses: HashMap<NodeId, FetchDocumentsResponse>,\n874\t    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n875\t    pub delay_ms: u64,\n876\t}\n877\t\n878\timpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n879\t    async fn search_node(\n880\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n881\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n882\t        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n883\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n884\t        Ok(self.responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n885\t            serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n886\t        }))\n887\t    }\n888\t\n889\t    async fn preflight_node(\n890\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n894\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n895\t        }))\n896\t    }\n897\t\n898\t    async fn write_documents(\n899\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n900\t    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n901\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n902\t        Ok(self.write_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n903\t            WriteResponse {\n904\t                success: true,\n905\t                task_uid: Some(1),\n906\t                message: None,\n907\t                code: None,\n908\t                error_type: None,\n909\t            }\n910\t        }))\n911\t    }\n912\t\n913\t    async fn delete_documents(\n914\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n915\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n916\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n917\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n918\t            success: true,\n919\t            task_uid:     &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n928\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n929\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) {            error_type: None,\n936\t        })\n937\t    }\n938\t\n939\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n940\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n941\t    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n942\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n943\t        // Return stored fetch response if available, otherwise return empty\n944\t        let stored = self.fetch_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n945\t            FetchDocumentsResponse {\n946\t                results: results: Vec::new(),\n957\t                limit: request.limit,\n958\t                offset: request.offset,\n959\t                total: stored.total,\n960\t            });\n961\t _request.task_uid;\n974\t        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n975\t        async move {\n976\t            if let Some(err) = error {\n977\t                return Err(err);\n978\t            }\n979\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n980\t                task_uid,\n981\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n982\t                error: None,\n983\t                error_type: None,\n984\t            })\n985\t        }\n986\t    }\n987\t}\n988\t\n989\t#[cfg(test)]\n990\tmod tests {\n991\t    use super::*;\n992\t    use crate::topology::{Node, make_test_topology();\n1092\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n1093\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1094\t        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n1095\t        let s = assert!(!r.degraded);\n1098\t    }\n1099\t\n1100\t    #[tokio::test]\n1101\t    async fn test_sg_degraded() {\n1102\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n1103\t        let plan = assert!(extract_query_terms(&None).is_empty());\n1115\t    }\n1116\t\n1117\t    #[test]\n1118\t    fn test_global_idf_aggregation() {\n1119\t        let resp = vec![\n1120\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]) },\n1121\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 60.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 4500 })]) },\n1122\t        ];\n1123\t        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&resp);\n1124\t        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n1125\t        assert!((g.avg_doc_length - 55.0).abs() < 0.001);\n1126\t        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"a\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n1127\t    }\n1128\t\n1129\t assert!(g.terms.is_empty());\n1134\t    }\n1135\t\n1136\t    #[tokio::test]\n1137\t    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n1138\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n1139\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n1140\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1141\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1142\t            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1143\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n1144\t        });\n1145\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1146\t            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n1147\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 2500 })]),\n1148\t        });\n1149\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1150\t            total_docs: 40000, avg_doc_length: 52.0,\n1151\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 4000 })]),\n1152\t        });\n1153\t        let req = PreflightRequest { index_uid: \"test\".into(), terms: vec![\"search\".into()], filter: None };\n1154\t        let g = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1155\t        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n1156\t        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"search\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n1157\t    }\n1158\t\n1159\t    #[tokio::test]\n1160\t    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n1161\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n1162\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n1163\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1164\t        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n1165\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1166\t            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1167\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n1168\t        });\n1169\t        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n1170\t        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n1171\t        assert!(!r.degraded);\n1172\t        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n1173\t    }\n1174\t\n1175\t    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n1176\t    ///\n1177\t    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n1178\t    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n1179\t    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n1180\t    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n1181\t    ///\n1182\t    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n1183\t    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1184\t    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1185\t    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n1186\t    ///\n1187\t    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n1188\t    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n1189\t    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1190\t    ///\n1191\t    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n1192\t    #[tokio::test]\n1193\t    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n1194\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n1195\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1196\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n1197\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n1198\t\n1199\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n1200\t\n1201\t        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n1202\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1203\t\n1204\t        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n1205\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1206\t            total_docs: 1000,\n1207\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1208\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1209\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n1210\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n1211\t            ]),\n1212\t        });\n1213\t\n1214\t        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n1215\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1216\t            total_docs: 100_000,\n1217\t            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n1218\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1219\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n1220\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n1221\t            ]),\n1222\t        });\n1223\t\n1224\t        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n1225\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1226\t            total_docs: 10,\n1227\t            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n1228\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1229\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1230\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1231\t            ]),\n1232\t        });\n1233\t\n1234\t        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n1235\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1236\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1237\t            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n1238\t            filter: None,\n1239\t        };\n1240\t\n1241\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1242\t\n1243\t        // Verify global aggregation\n1244\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n1245\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n1246\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n1247\t\n1248\t        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n1249\t        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n1250\t        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1251\t        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n1252\t        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n1253\t\n1254\t        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n1255\t        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1256\t        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n1257\t        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n1258\t\n1259\t        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n1260\t        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n1261\t        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n1262\t        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n1263\t        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1264\t        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1265\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n1266\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n1267\t    }\n1268\t\n1269\t    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n1270\t    #[tokio::test]\n1271\t    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n1272\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n1273\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n1274\t        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1275\t\n1276\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1277\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1278\t            terms: vec![],\n1279\t            filter: None,\n1280\t        };\n1281\t\n1282\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1283\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n1284\t        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n1285\t    }\n1286\t\n1287\t    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n1288\t    #[tokio::test]\n1289\t    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n1290\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n1291\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1292\t that to_node_body correctly injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n1328\t    #[test]\n1329\t    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n1330\t        let req = SearchRequest {\n1331\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1332\t            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n1333\t            offset: 10,\n1334\t            limit: 20,\n1335\t            filter: Some(serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")),\n1336\t            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into(), \"tags\".into()]),\n1337\t            ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n1338\t            body: serde_json::json!({\"custom\": \"field\"}),\n1339\t            global_idf: None,\n1340\t        };\n1341\t\n1342\t        let body = req.to_node_body();\n1343\t\n1344\t        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n1345\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n1346\t\n1347\t        // limit must be offset + limit (coordinator pagination)\n1348\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(30)));\n1349\t\n1350\t        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n1351\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n1352\t\n1353\t        // query must be set\n1354\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"q\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"rust programming\")));\n1355\t\n1356\t        // filter must be set\n1357\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"filter\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")));\n1358\t\n1359\t        // facets must be set\n1360\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"facets\"), Some(&serde_json::json!([\"category\", \"tags\"])));\n1361\t\n1362\t        // custom body field must be preserved\n1363\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"custom\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"field\")));\n1364\t    }\n1365\t\n1366\t    /// Test that to_node_body works with minimal request.\n1367\t    #[test]\n1368\t    fn test_to_node_body_minimal_request() {\n1369\t        let req = SearchRequest {\n1370\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1371\t            query:        let req = SearchRequest {\n1397\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1398\t            query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n1399\t            offset: 100,\n1400\t            limit: 50,\n1401\t            filter: None,\n1402\t            facets: None,\n1403\t            ranking_score: false,\n1404\t            body: serde_json::json!({}),\n1405\t            global_idf: None,\n1406\t        };\n1407\t\n1408\t        let body = req.to_node_body();\n1409\t\n1410\t        // limit must be offset + limit = 150 (fetch enough for coordinator pagination)\n1411\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(150)));\n1412\t\n1413\t        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n1414\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n1415\t    }\n1416\t\n1417\t    /// Test group fallback when primary group has failed nodes.\n1418\t    #[tokio::test]\n1419\t    async fn test_group_fallback_on_partial_failure() {\n1420\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n1421\t        // Group 0: 2 nodes\n1422\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1423\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n1424\t        // Group 1: 2 nodes (healthy fallback targets)\n1425\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n1426\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n1427\t\n1428\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await; // query_seq=0 → group 0\n1429\t        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n1430\t\n1431\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1432\t\n1433\t        // Set up responses: all shards on group 1 nodes return valid data\n1434\t        let response_1 = serde_json::json!({\n1435\t            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}],\n1436\t            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n1437\t            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n1438\t        });\n1439\t        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), response_1.clone());\n1440\t        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), response_1);\n1441\t\n1442\t        // All nodes in group 0 fail\n1443\t        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n1444\t        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n1445\t\n1446\t        let req = make_req();\n1447\t\n1448\t        // With fallback policy, shards should succeed via group 1\n1449\t        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n1450\t\n1451\t        // Should have succeeded via fallback (group 1)\n1452\t        assert!(!result.partial, \"Fallback should have succeeded\");\n1453\t        assert!(result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"No shards should have failed after fallback\");\n1454\t        assert!(!result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Should have shard pages from fallback\");\n1455\t    }\n1456\t\n1457\t    /// Test group fallback when both groups are down.\n1458\t    #[tokio::test]\n1459\t    async fn test_group_fallback_all_groups_down() {\n1460\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n1461\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1462\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n1463\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n1464\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n1465\t\n1466\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await;\n1467\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1468\t\n1469\t        // All nodes fail\n1470\t        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n1471\t        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n1472\t Scatter orchestration: fan-out logic and covering set builder.\n\nuse crate::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse crate::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector;\nuse tracing::{instrument, info_span, ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Per-term document frequency from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n}\n\n/// Preflight request: gather term-frequency statistics from a shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub terms: Vec<String>,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a shard's preflight query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightResponse {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub term_stats: HashMap<String, TermStats>,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global term statistics after coordinator aggregation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalTermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n    pub idf: f64,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global IDF data computed at the coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalIdf {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub terms: HashMap<String, GlobalTermStats>,\n}\n\nimpl GlobalIdf {\n    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n        for resp in responses {\n            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n            }\n        }\n\n        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n            total_length / total_docs as f64\n        } else {\n            0.0\n        };\n\n        let n = total_docs as f64;\n        let terms = term_df\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(term, df)| {\n                let idf = if df == 0 {\n                    0.0\n                } else {\n                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n                };\n                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Write path: document operations crate::task::NodeTaskStatus {\n        match self.status.as_str() {\n            \"enqueued\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            \"processing\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing,\n            \"succeeded\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded,\n            \"failed\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed,\n            _ => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// HTTP client for communicating with a Meilisearch node.\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\npub trait NodeClient: Send +
